description: §51.0.H Form 1 — the `effect=` attribute is legal ONLY on a SINGLE-target `rule=`. Here `.Title` has a multi-target `rule=(.Playing | .Paused)` AND an `effect=`, which is ambiguous (which target fires the effect?) → E-ENGINE-EFFECT-AMBIGUOUS. This is the negative-space of the `on-transition-to` runtime case (which uses the multi-target Form 2 `<onTransition to=.X>` instead precisely because effect= cannot disambiguate). The SPEC-sanctioned fix is to move the effect into a `<onTransition to=.Playing>` child.
rationale: §51.0.H Form 1: `effect=` is legal ONLY when `rule=` is single-target; combining `effect=` with a multi-target `rule=` is ambiguous → E-ENGINE-EFFECT-AMBIGUOUS. The multi-target case must use Form 2 `<onTransition to=.Variant>` children, which name the specific edge per effect.
